title: Provider Catalog File
description: Schema for a single mchatai-source/llm-catalog/providers/<id>.json file. Each file enumerates the LLM models available from one provider, including pricing, capabilities, and lifecycle status. Read by mChatAI+ (macOS + iOS), mchataiweb Cloud Functions, and LLMCostTracker at runtime.

$schema.description: Optional schema pointer; convention is the relative path '../schema/provider.schema.json'.

providerID.description: Stable lowercase identifier. MUST match the filename: providers/<providerID>.json.

apiBaseURL.description: The HTTPS endpoint used for chat completions. Provider-specific; not all are OpenAI-compatible.

apiStyle.description: Which request/response shape the consumer should use. Many third-party providers (xAI, Mistral, Together, DeepSeek, Perplexity, OpenRouter) speak the 'openai' style.

keychainKey.description: macOS/iOS Keychain key under which the API key is stored. Ignored by Node consumers (which use Firebase Functions secrets).

defaultModel.description: Must be the 'name' of one of the 'models[]' entries whose status is 'active'.

lastSyncedAt.description: ISO yyyy-mm-dd. When the cron last touched this file (or when it was hand-edited).

syncSource.description: 'cron' = scheduledModelCatalogSync may rewrite this file. 'hand-maintained' = cron leaves it alone (Apple Foundation, Ollama, etc.).

name.description: The provider's SKU as accepted by their API (e.g. 'gemini-2.5-flash', 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout-17B-16E-Instruct'). Unique within this provider file.

status.description: 'active' = pickers should offer it. 'deprecated' = still callable, picker may show a 'deprecated' badge, planner may avoid. 'retired' = no longer reachable; consumers MUST resolve to 'replacedBy'.

addedAt.description: ISO yyyy-mm-dd when this model first appeared in the catalog. Optional for legacy entries; required when the cron inserts a new model.

deprecatedAt.description: Only present when status is 'deprecated' or 'retired'.

replacedBy.description: The 'name' of another model (same or different provider, written as 'providerID:name' if cross-provider) that consumers should fall back to. REQUIRED when status is 'retired'.

inputPerMillion.description: USD per 1M input tokens. 0 for on-device / free models. May be 0 if pricingSource is 'unknown' — consumers should treat that as conservative-ceiling rather than 'free'.

pricingSource.description: Where the pricing came from. Optional; defaults to 'hand-maintained' for seed entries. 'on-device' applies to Apple Foundation.

costTier.description: Rough cost bucket used by the picker UI.

equivalentTier.description: Which escalation-ladder rung this model occupies. Used for cross-provider failover.

capabilities.description: Feature flags consumers gate on. 'thinking' (Gemini-style extended-thinking budgets) is distinct from 'reasoning' (o-series, R1, Sonar-Reasoning).

contextTokens.description: Maximum total context window (input tokens). Optional; consumers use this to bound retrieval/truncation.

maxOutputTokens.description: Provider-imposed cap on output tokens. Optional; LLMGenerateSkill's llmMaxTokens is still clamped 64-32000 by the consumer.

notes.description: Free-form humans-only context (e.g. 'Gemini's thinking tokens count against maxTokens — budget 800+ for validation'). Never used for behavior.
